rows.design_decision: Introduce MRI-inspired k-space angle frequency analysis and dual physical masking (medium and angular direction) to model light-matter interactions.
rows.motivation: Traditional Epipolar Plane Image (EPI) methods fail catastrophically on non-Lambertian surfaces because specular and scattered reflections violate the viewpoint consistency assumption, creating parallax errors that are falsely interpreted as depth.
rows.sota_gap: Moves beyond treating reflections as noise to be filtered; instead, it leverages them as physical priors by mapping reflections via 2D-DFT into angle frequency spectra (low freq = diffuse, high freq = specular, mid freq = scattering), explicitly solving Maxwell's equations and BRDF parameters.
rows.scenario: Scenes with complex, mixed-material objects causing severe multi-path reflections and scattering, where standard EPI depth estimation completely fails.
rows.evidence: Theoretical derivation of solving 3 unknown BRDF weights using 81 angle samples via constrained least squares, successfully decoupling physical reflection types.
rows.section: Methodology: Physical Modeling and Frequency Analysis

rows.design_decision: Implement an adaptive dual-modal energy map optimization strategy driven by Geometric Consistency Descriptors (GCD) and global/local subtraction rules.
rows.motivation: Pure EPI-based architectures cannot distinguish between true depth discontinuities and high-energy specular artifacts, resulting in background texture copying and depth blurring at object boundaries.
rows.sota_gap: Replaces static thresholding with dynamic pixel-level adaptivity: uses $\alpha_{local} = \alpha_{global} \times (G / G.mean())$ for localized contrast enhancement when GCD and energy maps are co-directional, massively amplifying the Non-Lambertian to Lambertian (NL/L) energy ratio.
rows.scenario: Highly textured backgrounds adjacent to transparent, highly reflective, or glossy non-Lambertian foreground objects.
rows.evidence: Quantitative energy ratio improvements documented in project brief breakthroughs: 320% ratio increase for the 'Apple' scene and 111% increase for the 'Teddy' scene.

rows.design_decision: Integrate Segment Anything Model (SAM) to extract ternary scene masks for differentiated energy suppression and enhancement.
rows.motivation: Uniformly applying energy subtraction risks eroding weak but true depth signals in rough Lambertian areas while inadequately suppressing strong smooth Lambertian glare.
rows.sota_gap: Combines foundational vision models (SAM) with physical light field priors to categorize regions into rough Lambertian, smooth Lambertian, and smooth non-Lambertian, applying surgical energy adjustments (e.g., 85% suppression for smooth Lambertian, 50% enhancement for non-Lambertian).
rows.scenario: Complex real-world environments with ambiguous material boundaries, requiring semantic-level understanding to guide physical depth calculations.
rows.evidence: Project brief Breakthrough 3 shows the SAM-guided adaptive switching strategy causes a massive 495.8% ratio explosion in the 'Apple' scene, providing extremely pure features.

rows.design_decision: Construct a 4D cost volume utilizing 100% of the available 81 viewpoints (MACVO-Net) instead of the standard rigid 28-viewpoint EPI structure.
rows.motivation: Relying on sparse, directionally rigid EPIs caps the geometric information available to the network, creating an accuracy ceiling (MAE stagnating at 0.28-0.34) and losing spatial context.
rows.sota_gap: Completely discards implicit EPI feature extraction. By warping all 80 non-central views across 32 disparity hypotheses and calculating photometric errors, it builds an explicit, geometry-complete 4D cost volume that captures holistic scene structure.
rows.scenario: Occluded edges and highly textured Lambertian regions where multi-view parallax cues must be maximized to achieve sub-pixel depth accuracy.
rows.evidence: Network architectural design processing the explicit 4D cost volume, effectively bypassing the 0.28-0.34 MAE accuracy ceiling inherent to 28-view EPI networks.

rows.design_decision: Use the naturally flat cost volume response in non-Lambertian regions as an explicit uncertainty signal to guide a U-Net multi-scale decoder.
rows.motivation: Specular and scattering surfaces lack low-disparity matching costs, causing standard depth regression networks to hallucinate random depths (texture copying) due to uniform feature distributions.
rows.sota_gap: Transforms a fundamental weakness (flat cost volume) into an explicit mathematical uncertainty prior. The U-Net decoder uses multi-scale skip connections to actively propagate depth from confident neighboring Lambertian pixels, rather than forcing a false estimation.
rows.scenario: Large contiguous non-Lambertian surface areas (e.g., mirrors, glossy metal, glass) where internal depth features are entirely unobservable.
rows.evidence: Successful mitigation of texture-copying artifacts in non-Lambertian zones, demonstrating mathematically sound depth completion via neighborhood propagation.

rows.design_decision: Design ablation studies isolating the physical energy optimization (GCD/SAM) from the network architectural changes (MACVO-Net).
rows.motivation: We must prove that the physical pre-processing (raising NL/L ratio) and the deep learning architecture (handling flat cost volumes) provide independent, synergistic benefits, rather than masking each other's failures.
rows.sota_gap: Disentangles the traditional end-to-end black box by providing explicit modular evaluation, proving that physical priors feed cleaner data while the network maximizes the utility of that clean data.
rows.scenario: Evaluation across standard light field benchmarks (e.g., HCI 4D Light Field Benchmark) containing both strict Lambertian and mixed-material scenes.
rows.evidence: Metrics will compare Baseline vs. +GCD/Energy Map vs. +SAM vs. MACVO-Net, measuring BadPix (BPR) and Mean Absolute Error (MAE) to isolate the exact contribution of each innovation point.
